## Summary

The Review Gate was blocking evaluations when the ledger quality report had `repair_required` status. This status means >3 advisory warnings but zero hard fails — missing pressure systems, ending accountability notes, underweighted characters, etc. These are informational findings, not critical failures.

"The Wonderful World of Oz" evaluation was hitting this: the ledger quality check generated 4+ advisory warnings (normal for a classic novel with many characters/symbols), causing the pipeline to terminate with `"Review Gate blocked: ledger_quality_report_v1 is repair_required and not reviewable yet."`

Now `repair_required` kicks forward (like `blocked_retryable_technical` already does). Warnings are preserved in the quality report artifact for downstream visibility but do not kill the evaluation.

## Scope

**Touches:**
- `lib/evaluation/phase-architecture-v2/gateValidity.ts` — `repair_required` returns `ok: true` (kick forward) instead of `ok: false` (block)
- `lib/evaluation/review-gate/storyLedgerApprovalNormalizer.ts` — allows `repair_required` through the approval check (same as `reviewable`)
- Two test files updated to expect kick-forward behavior

**Does NOT touch:**
- `buildLedgerQualityReport.ts` — the threshold logic (`>3 warnings = repair_required`) is unchanged; the status is still computed and stored
- Pipeline phases, chunk logic, scoring, prompts
- Any UI components

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- `repair_required` was previously a hard block. Kicking forward means manuscripts with 4+ advisory warnings will now complete evaluations instead of failing. This is the correct behavior — advisory warnings should never terminate an evaluation.
- Hard fails (`blocked_content_hard_fail`) and true blocks (`blocked`) are unchanged and still stop the pipeline.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: N/A — this IS a pipeline gate change, intentionally allowing advisory-warning evaluations to complete.
